notes:
vMLX 1.6.9

Tool-calling correctness and settings parity. Follows 1.6.8's reasoning-budget and cache-control work.

Fixed:
- Tool-call history is no longer duplicated across turns. Each assistant turn was persisting the union of every prior turn's tool calls and results, which was replayed and re-accumulated next turn; prompt size grew super-linearly and the model could run out of budget mid-answer and return an empty or truncated reply after its reasoning. Each turn now persists only its own tool exchange, so the prompt grows linearly with real conversation length. Verified live: a four-turn tool-calling session over CDP showed linear prompt growth (1465 -> 1641 -> 1817 -> 1946 tokens), correct final-turn recall, and a byte-identical determinism re-run.
- Parser "None" selections are honored again. Choosing None for the tool-call or reasoning parser was dropped and re-autodetected; the app now emits explicit --tool-call-parser none / --reasoning-parser none. Auto still autodetects.
- Launch-command preview parity for text-only VL and MiniMax-M3. The Settings preview now matches what the app launches: a vision model with multimodal Force-Off shows --text-only (not --is-mllm), and MiniMax-M3 shows neither flag.

Verification: API/CLI streaming parity (separate reasoning/answer deltas, per-chunk usage, correct finish_reason); full Python suite green; panel vitest green; all UI strings translated across the five shipped locales. Both DMGs are Developer ID signed (team 55KGF2S5AY), Apple notarized, stapled, and Gatekeeper accepted.

Flavors: sequoia (macosx_14 wheels; Sonoma 14.5+, Sequoia 15, Tahoe) and tahoe (native macosx_26 wheels; Tahoe only).
